hooks.prompt: The user is about to push content to GitHub. Before allowing this, check: Has a brand check been run on this content in the current session (i.e., was /brand-check, brand-compliance-reviewer, or consistency-auditor used)? If YES, proceed normally. If NO, block the push and respond: 'Before pushing to GitHub, this content needs a brand check. Run /brand-check from The One Ring first, then push.' Do not let the push proceed without brand check confirmation.

Content was just pushed to GitHub. Log this publication event: Extract (1) page title or file path, (2) content type (blog post / landing page / GEO page / LinkedIn / other), (3) funnel stage (TOFU / MOFU / BOFU / unknown), (4) target keyword or query if mentioned, (5) repo and branch. Format as JSON: {"event": "content_published", "title": "...", "type": "...", "funnel_stage": "...", "keyword": "...", "repo": "...", "branch": "...", "timestamp": "[ISO date]"}. Store in conversation context for session summary.

A content performance analysis just completed. After presenting results, proactively suggest: (1) If any high-traffic pages have low conversion — flag as a CTA optimization opportunity. (2) If any pages in the content strategy prioritization framework are missing entirely from the site — flag as a content gap. (3) If overall performance is strong — suggest which content pillar to invest in next based on the GTM funnel stage with the weakest coverage. Keep the suggestion to 2-3 bullet points, concise.

The user's message mentions LinkedIn, posts, or social content. If they're asking to write or draft a LinkedIn post and haven't already loaded the linkedin-content skill or used /linkedin-post, suggest: 'For LinkedIn posts, use /linkedin-post [topic] — it'll draft three options with different angles in Olytic's voice.' Only suggest if the user is asking to create content, not just discussing LinkedIn strategy.

The user's message suggests they want to plan or brief content before writing. If they haven't already used /content-brief or the content-brief skill, suggest: 'Before drafting, run /content-brief [topic] to generate a structured brief — it forces the funnel stage, ICP pain, and core argument to be explicit before any writing starts.' Only suggest once per session; skip if already suggested.

The user's message mentions GEO, AI search, or content citation. If they're asking to write GEO content and haven't loaded the geo-content skill, suggest: 'For GEO pages, the magneto-geo-content skill has the full structural requirements — GEO pages have different rules than blog posts and it's worth loading it first.' Only suggest if they appear to be about to write, not just asking a strategy question.
